data.description:
VERIFIED LIVE (incident, remediated): an MCP reconcile call with mode=bootstrap-strict from a sandboxed temp store, with REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1, executed against real Jira and created 19 real issues (DIG-5615..DIG-5633; all deleted and deletion verified by JQL). The readonly gate and the live gate both failed to stop a Jira-mutating pass.

Root cause: mcp_server.py:271-276 gates ONLY mode == "live" behind REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE; rebar_reconciler/mode.py:106-112 MODE_CAPS gives BOOTSTRAP_STRICT=10 and BOOTSTRAP_THROTTLE=100 APPLIED mutations per pass (only RECONCILE_CHECK/DRY_RUN are cap-0). The reconcile tool also sits outside the _readonly() write gate (mcp_server.py: write section starts after it). Note dry-run is not a pure read either: it writes bridge_state/ and the invariants path files local bug tickets.

Expected: any Jira-mutating mode requires REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE=1; a REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1 server exposes at most reconcile-check/dry-run (arguably nothing mutating local state either).

Fix direction: gate Mode.from_str(mode) > Mode.DRY_RUN behind the env var; restrict the tool (or its modes) under _readonly(); correct the docstrings + CLAUDE.md ("'live' mutates Jira" is dangerously incomplete — also __init__.py:404-409).

RED test: parametrize all five modes x {readonly, non-readonly} x {with, without REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_RECONCILE_LIVE}; assert only non-mutating modes pass without the env var and that readonly blocks mutating modes entirely (use a fake acli on PATH; never real credentials in tests).
data.title: MCP reconcile: bootstrap modes mutate real Jira ungated, even under REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1
